title: crataegus orientalis subsp. presliana
intro: Crataegus orientalis subsp. presliana, also known as the Oriental Hawthorn, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardioprotective effects. The health benefits of Crataegus orientalis subsp. presliana are attributed to its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce the risk of heart disease. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as tinctures and teas, are commonly used to promote cardiovascular well-being and alleviate symptoms of hypertension and arrhythmia.
